title: ACLS Exam - Advanced Cardiovascular Life Support
description: Advanced Cardiovascular Life Support exam questions from NREMT database

questions.text: The single most important factor in determining survival from cardiac arrest is:
What is the preferred technique for providing chest compression during 2-rescuer CPR for an infant
One likely cause of the bradycardia is:
A medication used to dilate the bronchioles and causes the blood vessels to constrict is:
The dosage epinephrine auto injector for child is:
Epinephrine is a medication used to constrict to the bronchioles and causes the blood vessels to dilate:
An EMT responds to a cardiac arrest. The patient Hasn't(DNR)order. The EMT should:
Excitability and anxiety, increased heart rate and headache, consider Side effects Epinephrine:
What modifications of the usual management of cardiac arrest should be made for a pregnant patient?
options: Deliver low-flow oxygen to prevent fetal oxygen toxicity
Administer vasopressors at double the usual dose
Perform cardiac compressions higher on the sternum
Deliver small boluses of crystalloids to prevent fetal pulmonary edema

questions.text: Identify the ecg

A 49-year-old male was crossing the street and struck by a truck. First responders are doing CPR. The patient is pulseless, apneic and the monitor shows asystole. Your assessment reveals an open skull fracture and deformed pelvis. Based on the ITLS curriculum, which of the following is the most appropriate act?
options: Immediately intubate the patient to secure the airway
Establish intravenous access and administer a 1 liter bolus
Establish intravenous access and administer 2 mg of atropine
Resuscitative efforts should be discontinued, unless prohibited by law

questions.text: When is it appropriate to use an AED in a witnessed pediatric cardiac arrest?
options: Once the patient is in the ambulance.
When the ambulance is en route to the hospital.

questions.text: A 3-month-old infant is found unresponsive in his crib and has cool, mottled skin. His vital signs are HR 50 and RR 6. What is the initial treatment for this patient?
options: Establish IV access and administer an IV fluid bolus of 20 mL/kg.
Establish IV access and administer 0.01 mg/kg IV epinephrine 1:10,000.
Perform chest compressions and ventilate with a bag-mask device.
Initiate bag-mask ventilation and then reassess his skin color and heart rate.

questions.text: What is the appropriate compression depth in a 6-month-old child in cardiac arrest?
What is the dysrhythmia for a 3-year-old with a heart rate of 180 beats/min, a narrow QRS complex, and a variable R-R interval called?
What is the dysrhythmia for a 16-year-old with a heart rate of 180 beats/min, a narrow QRS complex, and a nonvariable RR interval?
A child has a heart rate between 180 - 188 beats/min with a narrow QRS and a strong pulse. Which of the following best describes his rhythm?
What is the dysrhythmia for a 16-year-old with a heart rate of 180 beats/min, a narrow QRS complex, and a non-variable RR interval?
Which of the following is most likely to present with hypertension and bradycardia?

A 70-year-old male presents responsive to painful stimulus only, with hot and dry skin. You note an infected bedsore on his buttock. After the administration of 1000 ml of normal saline, his vital signs remain unchanged at HR = 90, BP = 56/?. Of the following which would be the most appropriate next action?

You are assessing a 69-year-old male, complaining of chest discomfort. His 12-Lead ECG reveals ST depression in leads V1-V4. There is no ST elevation in any lead. This ECG finding is most consistent with:
Symptomatic hyperkalemia should be treated with which of the following medication combinations?
options: Sodium bicarbonate, naloxone, and calcium chloride

questions.text: A 62-year-old female presents with an acute onset of weakness and dizziness. Vital signs are HR = 52, BP = 240/160, RR = 18. Which of the following actions is the most appropriate?
Your patient is presenting with an acute onset of itchiness and urticaria after eating peanuts. Of the following options, which is the most appropriate treatment?
options: None, this is a mild reaction

questions.text: A patient presents hemodynamically stable complaining of chest discomfort. A 12-Lead ECG reveals ST segment elevation in all the precordial leads as well as II, III, and aVF. Which of the following is the most likely etiology of these ECG findings?

A 52-year-old male presents complaining of chest discomfort. He is alert and oriented with a blood pressure of 110/70. You obtain the 12-Lead ECG, Shows an Inferior Wall MI (ST elevation in II, III, aVF). Of the following, the most appropriate next action would be to:
You are dispatched to a soccer game for a 12-year-old female with signs and symptoms of an allergic reaction. Upon arrival, the patient mother hands you an epinephrine auto-injector. You should:
options: Direct the mother to administer the epi-pen
Contact the physician that prescribed the epi-pen

questions.text: You are treating a 35-year-old man who was stung by a bee. He is allergic to bees and has a prescribed epinephrine auto-injector. His breath sounds are clear and non-labored. His BP is 130/82. How should you manage this patient?
options: Administer the epinephrine and transport rapidly
Begin immediate transport and administer the epinephrine en route
Assess the patient for signs of hypoxia, administer oxygen as needed, and transport
Let medical control know your anaphylactic patient needs his epinephrine

questions.text: An elderly patient reportedly collapsed on a golf course almost 10 minutes ago. You assess her level of consciousness and determine she is unresponsive. Which of the following should you do first:
options: Open the airway and insert an OPA
Assess pulse and begin CPR if needed
Providing ventilations with high flow oxygen

questions.text: You are caring for a 3-year-old patient in cardiac arrest. What is the correct compression to ventilation ratio for two-person CPR on this patient?

A 23-year-old female fell from a second-floor balcony. Upon arrival, you find her lying in the grass. She responds to verbal commands and your assessment reveals flat neck veins, and normal chest, abdomen and pelvis examinations. Her skin is cool, clammy and ashen; respirations are rapid and shallow; radial pulses are too rapid to count and thready. You place her on the heart monitor and it shows a wide-complex tachycardia of about 280 per minute. You should suspect:

Your patient is complaining of an acute onset of chest discomfort and shortness of breath. His vital signs are P = 116, RR = 18, BP = 108/63. His skin is pale, cool, and diaphoretic. Based on this presentation, the patient is most likely experiencing?

A 34-year-old female presents with dyspnea, audible wheezing and responds to painful stimulus. Her husband states that she used her inhaler multiple times without relief. Of the following, the best treatment would be?
